testing_strategy: Use a fixture for Move Express controllers to thin adapter style with hexagonal-friendly use cases that exercises the owned boundary, then add: happy-path test, unauthorized/invalid-input test, retry or concurrency simulation, persistence/contract assertion, and rollback/regression test. Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-199 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Move Express controllers to thin adapter style with hexagonal-friendly use cases handles the demo path but misses the architecture migration boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and render commit time are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.

title: Build typed event bus replacing callback-based component communication
business_context: Components communicate via prop-drilled callbacks across deep trees. Adopt typed pub/sub bus for cross-tree events.

files.content: export function Notifications({ onClick }: { onClick: () => void }) { return <button onClick={onClick}>Notify</button>; }

reasoning: Module-scoped emitter with typed events map. useEvent hook subscribes with cleanup. Components emit and listen without prop-drilling.

files.purpose: Typed bus with subscribe returning cleanup

files.content:
import { emit } from '../lib/event-bus';
export function Notifications({ id }: { id: string }) { return <button onClick={() => emit('notification:click', { id })}>Notify</button>; }

testing_strategy: Use a fixture for Build typed event bus replacing callback-based component communication that exercises the owned boundary, then add: happy-path test, unauthorized/invalid-input test, retry or concurrency simulation, persistence/contract assertion, and rollback/regression test. Assert the observable output, database/cache side effect, emitted event or log, and one failure metric. Run the suite in CI with deterministic seeds and a production-shaped fixture so fs-200 cannot pass through a mocked happy path only.
what_breaks_naive_approach: A naive solution for Build typed event bus replacing callback-based component communication handles the demo path but misses the state overhaul boundary: authorization, persistence, reconnect or retry behavior, rollback, and policy block rate are not proved under production-shaped fixtures.
